findings.title: Need for Notification Priority Levels
findings.description: Users require a system to distinguish between critical alerts and routine updates. The current uniformity in notification presentation leads to important messages being overlooked. A three-tier priority system is suggested to visually differentiate urgency.
assumptions: Users are overwhelmed by the current notification system.
A tiered priority system will improve user experience.

evidence.excerpt: Users need a way to distinguish between critical alerts and routine updates.
Sarah described the experience as 'drowning in noise'... transactional alerts carry the same visual weight as critical incidents.

recommendations: Implement a three-tier notification priority system.

findings.title: Granular Notification Preferences Needed
findings.description: There is a strong demand for users to have granular control over notification preferences, allowing configuration per team and individual overrides. This feature is crucial for managing notification overload and ensuring compliance with user needs.
assumptions: Users want to customize their notification settings to reduce noise.
Granular preferences will enhance user satisfaction.

evidence.excerpt: Users should be able to opt in/out of specific notification categories.
Priya's top request is granular notification preferences.

recommendations: Develop a user preference page for notification categories.

findings.title: Mobile Notification Delivery Issues
findings.description: A significant portion of users on Android 14 are experiencing silent failures in push notifications, leading to missed alerts. This issue is critical as it affects user retention and satisfaction.
assumptions: Users rely on mobile notifications for timely updates.
Silent failures will drive users to competitors.

evidence.excerpt: Several users on Android 14 report that push notifications silently fail.
Marcus mentioned he frequently misses push notifications on his Pixel 8.

recommendations: Investigate and resolve push notification delivery issues on Android 14.

findings.title: Desire for Digest-Style Summaries
findings.description: Users prefer receiving low-priority notifications in a digest format rather than as individual alerts. This would help reduce notification fatigue and improve engagement.
assumptions: Digest-style summaries will enhance user experience.

evidence.excerpt: Marcus wants digest-style summaries instead of individual pings for non-urgent updates.

recommendations: Implement ML-based notification batching for low-priority alerts.

findings.title: Accessibility Concerns with Notification Center
findings.description: There are significant accessibility issues reported by users, particularly those using screen readers and colorblind individuals. This indicates a need for improved design to ensure all users can effectively navigate the notification center.
assumptions: Users with disabilities are struggling to use the current notification system.
Improving accessibility will enhance overall user satisfaction.

evidence.excerpt: Priya mentioned accessibility issues: several team members using screen readers have difficulty navigating the notification center.

recommendations: Conduct an accessibility audit and redesign the notification center.

summary: The analysis reveals critical insights into user needs for SmartNotify, particularly around notification prioritization, granular preferences, and mobile delivery issues. Users express a strong desire for improved accessibility and digest-style notifications to combat fatigue. These findings highlight significant opportunities for product enhancement.
